neighborhoods.name: Capitole / Centre
neighborhoods.description: The historic heart of Toulouse centered on the grand Place du Capitole, with pink brick architecture, theatres, and vibrant cafe culture
neighborhoods.character: Urban core, walkable, culturally rich, bustling

housing.predominantType: Apartments in historic pink-brick Haussmann-style buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Metro lines A and B intersect here. Weekly markets, excellent dining. Can be noisy -- student population keeps nightlife active.

neighborhoods.name: Saint-Cyprien
neighborhoods.description: Left-bank neighborhood across the Garonne from the center, with a village feel, art galleries, and the Abattoirs modern art museum
neighborhoods.character: Artsy, relaxed, increasingly trendy, river views

housing.predominantType: Mix of renovated older apartments and newer residences

neighborhoods.character_notes: Sunday morning market at Place Saint-Cyprien. Easy walk across Pont Neuf to the center. More authentic neighborhood feel than Capitole.

neighborhoods.name: Carmes
neighborhoods.description: Charming neighborhood south of Place du Capitole, centered on the covered Carmes market hall with narrow medieval streets
neighborhoods.character: Intimate historic, gourmet, residential calm near the center

housing.predominantType: Character apartments in 17th-19th century brick townhouses

neighborhoods.character_notes: The Marche des Carmes is one of the best covered markets in France. Quieter than Capitole but steps away. Popular with professionals and retirees.

neighborhoods.name: Cote Pavee
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential neighborhood east of the center on gentle hills, known for green spaces, schools, and quiet tree-lined streets
neighborhoods.character: Suburban-feel within the city, family-oriented, leafy, calm

housing.predominantType: Houses with gardens and mid-century apartment buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: Close to Jardin des Plantes and Canal du Midi. Good local bakeries and shops. Bus connections to Metro line B.

neighborhoods.name: Tournefeuille
neighborhoods.description: Suburban commune west of Toulouse with parks, modern amenities, and easy access to the Airbus employment zone
neighborhoods.character: Quiet suburb, spacious, modern, good value

housing.predominantType: Detached and semi-detached houses with gardens, some newer apartment complexes

neighborhoods.character_notes: One of the most popular suburbs for families and retirees. Large supermarkets, medical facilities, and parks. 20 minutes to city center by bus/car.
